text.title: Post-Training Evaluation and Promotion Decision
text.summary: Test set protocol, LLM-as-judge evaluation pattern with its limitations, deterministic metric alternatives, and the human-approval gate for production deployment.

rule.statement: Hold out a test set (separate from validation) that is never used during training or hyperparameter tuning — it is the only unbiased post-training quality signal.
rule.rationale: Validation data used for early stopping is contaminated; evaluating on it produces inflated scores. A held-out test set reveals real generalization quality.

rule.statement: Run the base model on the held-out test set before fine-tuning to establish a baseline — without it you cannot measure the improvement delta.
rule.rationale: Fine-tuning job cost is only justified if the improvement over the base model is measurable and exceeds the inference cost premium of the fine-tuned model.

rule.statement: If improvement over base model is less than 10% on the test set, reconsider fine-tuning — the cost premium may not be justified.
rule.rationale: Fine-tuned gpt-4o-mini costs 3-6x more per token than base gpt-4o-mini; marginal quality improvement does not justify this cost increase.

rule.statement: Do not use GPT-4o to judge GPT-4o-mini outputs as the sole evaluation criterion — prefer deterministic metrics (exact_match, format_compliance) when possible.
rule.rationale: GPT-4o-to-GPT-4o-mini judgment has circular bias; the evaluator has self-preference and may inflate or deflate scores for outputs similar to its own style.

rule.statement: Cap LLM-judge evaluation at 50-100 examples for routine post-training checks — each judge call costs money.
rule.rationale: Full-set LLM judge eval at 1000 examples can cost as much as the training run itself; sample-based eval provides sufficient signal for routine deployment decisions.

rule.statement: The decision to promote a fine-tuned model to production must be a human checkpoint — agents should write the model ID to a config file and stop; a human approves by updating the production config.
rule.rationale: Automated promotion bypasses human review of quality, cost, and safety tradeoffs. Fine-tuning decisions have lasting production impact that requires human accountability.

text.reference: LLM judge prompts for format_match scoring and promotion decision
